## 📝 Description

Please provide a detailed summary of the changes introduced by this Pull Request and link the corresponding issue.

Closes # (issue number)

---

## 🛠️ Type of Change

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update (non-breaking documentation/comment change)
- [ ] 🛡️ Security enhancement (patching vulnerability or improving configuration)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance optimization (improving speed, page loading, or memory footprint)
- [ ] 🤖 GitHub Actions / Workflow automation

---

## 🧪 Testing and Verification

### Environments Tested
- [ ] OS: Windows / macOS / Linux
- [ ] Browser: Chrome / Firefox / Safari / Edge
- [ ] Node.js Version: 

### Verification Steps
1. Describe step-by-step how to reproduce or verify your changes.
2. Provide details of the testing environment and mock data used.

## 📸 Visual Proof (if applicable)

Please attach screenshots or screen recordings (animations/videos) showing the before and after states of the user interface.

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
|        |       |

---

## 🔒 Security Checklist

- [ ] No API keys, credentials, or sensitive tokens are exposed/hardcoded.
- [ ] Input data is validated and sanitized to prevent injection/XSS.
- [ ] Secure communication practices and appropriate headers are used.

---

## 📋 Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the style and linting guidelines of this project.
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code.
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas.
- [ ] My changes do not generate any new runtime warnings or console errors.
- [ ] Responsive design has been verified across mobile, tablet, and desktop layouts.
- [ ] Accessibility standards (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus indicators) are met.
- [ ] Unit or integration tests have been run and all pass.
